title: Exercise test result review
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.
assessment.rationale: The NICE stable chest-pain section supports documenting the reason and context for reviewed diagnostic testing, symptoms and risk context, and clinician interpretation of an existing exercise-related result without automatically ordering a test or diagnosing ischemia.

source.title: Recent-onset chest pain of suspected cardiac origin: assessment and diagnosis
source.population: Adults with recent-onset acute or stable chest pain or discomfort suspected to be cardiac in origin.
source.setting: Initial, urgent, primary, outpatient, and specialist assessment of recent-onset chest pain.
source.exclusions: Exact for clinician-documented chest-pain history, associated symptoms, risk context, examination and reviewed tests, information, and safety-net discussion. It is old and only partial for atypical or established non-acute follow-up; diagnosis, urgency, testing, treatment, and referral decisions are not generated.

exact_location.heading: Section 1.3 - stable chest-pain assessment and investigation context

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documented stable-pain characteristics, risk and prior-investigation context, and clinician-selected or reviewed investigation discussion without automatic testing or diagnosis.

assessment.text: Supports documenting concerns, uncertainty, questions, explanation of reviewed tests, shared decisions, and clinician-provided advice for recurrent pain without generating patient instructions.
assessment.rationale: The information section supports documenting clinician explanation of a reviewed result, uncertainty, questions, shared decisions, safety-netting, and arranged follow-up without generating advice.
